blockingIssues.description: The completed worker session ac635f6f-96c0-4e74-80be-0b3346e911d4 has neither a handoff artifact nor a worker-transcripts.jsonl entry, so scrutiny cannot verify the declared commit, verification commands, or transcript skeleton required by the review contract.

The feature contract required ordinary colon strings to remain safe across save/get/get_record, but ordinary_colon_strings_are_not_record_links only proves save() and get(), leaving get_record() without evidence.

The reviewed commit 3ff4d10 only changes CRUD-surface docs/comments and generated convenience methods; it does not introduce or modify the stored-row RecordId decode seam promised by this feature, so there is no implementation diff to validate.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded that schema-managed inventory application and schemaless first-save/bootstrap are separately proven contracts and should not be treated as shared evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen rust-library-worker enforcement around isolating staged changes before commit, since the current procedure already asks workers to inspect git status --short, unstage unrelated changes, and avoid committing pre-existing staged artifacts, but a reviewed feature still committed unrelated .factory/validation files from other milestones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for milestone-c-store-schemaless-bootstrap found commit 83c9d237255cc46b5b665102e286613e0f6e012f included unrelated .factory/validation files despite the existing staging-isolation guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When decode logic is tightened for string-form record ids, require at least one regression using a non-test-specific or multiple table-name shapes so implementations cannot hard-code the it_record_* naming pattern and still satisfy review coverage.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for milestone-c-strict-record-link-string-classification found core/tests/integration_db.rs only exercised it_record_user:x and it_record_user:y, which allowed core/src/lib.rs to special-case the it_record_* prefix while leaving other legitimate table:key shapes unsupported.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how rust-library-worker should behave when an assigned feature is already satisfied by the checked-out mission baseline, so verification-only outcomes can still be reported without looking like procedure violations or being attached to non-implementing commits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for milestone-c-field-aware-record-id-decode-seam and milestone-c-typed-stored-row-record-id-decode-seam both encountered baseline-already-landed behavior, but only one had enough evidence to pass; the other was attached to commit 3ff4d10 even though that commit did not implement the seam.

rejectedObservations.observation: Store-wide schemaless bootstrap should be documented as shared architecture knowledge.
Missing mission handoff/transcript artifacts should be applied directly as a library or services update.
rejectedObservations.reason: reject: artifact-traceability gaps are mission-process problems that belong to orchestrator or skill guidance, not factual repo/library state
